Ready
Help & Documentation

Overview

PenOpt calculates the optimal scan orientation for industrial X-ray CT. Given a 3D mesh (STL or OBJ), it searches over tilt angles (θ, φ) to minimize X-ray penetration length and energy consumption.

How It Works

1. Upload a mesh — the app builds a BVH for fast ray intersection.

2. Configure beam energy, Tmin, pre-filter, scanner geometry.

3. Select a material from the NIST XCOM database (40+ materials).

4. Run optimization — casts rays through the mesh, coarse grid (49 orientations) followed by fine search.

5. Interpret results — optimal angles, heatmap, contour plot, energy recommendation.

Objective Functions

  • fmtl — Generalized mean of lengths (cube-root, m=3). Lower = less artifact.
  • fhdn — Projection area range. Lower = less beam hardening variation.

Keyboard Shortcuts

  • Ctrl+O — Open file
  • Ctrl+Enter — Start optimization
  • Esc — Dismiss error/help
  • 1/2/3 — View: 3D / Heatmap / Compare
  • R — Reset camera
  • F — Fullscreen
